topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

Sarah bounced in her chair, a bright, almost frantic energy vibrating through her. "You won't believe this," she exclaimed, practically leaping to her feet. Her eyes, wide and sparkling, darted around the office as if expecting someone to materialize with vital information. "I just found a faded photo. It’s from... Camp Whispering Pines." Mark, who had been quietly reviewing spreadsheets, looked up, a slow smile spreading across his face. "Camp Whispering Pines? That sounds… nostalgic." Sarah practically vibrated with excitement, tapping the photo with a quick finger. "It was last summer! I was there. And look, this is your face!" She thrust the picture toward him, her breath catching slightly as she waited for his reaction. Mark took the photo, his gaze lingering on the smiling face in the snapshot. A sudden spark ignited in his own eyes, a similar quickening of pace replacing his usual calm demeanor. "No way," he murmured, his voice dropping slightly in pitch. "I remember that. I was there too."

[story 2]

Liam couldn't sit still. He kept fiddling with the edge of his desk blotter, his fingers tracing invisible patterns. A thrill shot through him, making his palms feel warm and slightly damp. He glanced over at Chloe, who was deep in conversation with a colleague, and suddenly his focus snapped to her. "Hey, Chloe," he called out, his voice carrying an unusual lift. "Have you ever been to Camp Whispering Pines?" Chloe turned, a look of mild surprise crossing her features. Liam felt a rush, a distinct internal stirring that made his shoulders straighten. "I... I think so," she replied, a hint of something unreadable in her tone. Liam’s heart gave a little jump. He leaned in slightly, his curiosity pulling him forward. "Really? That's amazing! I was there last summer. It was incredible." He watched her expression shift, a flicker of recognition dawning in her eyes, and a profound sense of anticipation settled over him, making him want to ask everything immediately.

[story 3]

Maria couldn't contain the feeling bubbling up inside her. She paced back and forth across the small conference room, her movements quick and restless. Every glance she cast toward David felt charged with unspoken possibility. "David," she started, her voice a little too bright, a rapid-fire delivery that betrayed her inner state. "Did you ever attend Camp Whispering Pines?" David looked up from his laptop, a slight furrow in his brow. Maria stopped pacing abruptly, planting her hands on the table. "Last summer! I spent the entire week there. It was such a whirlwind of activities." A genuine grin broke across her face, and she leaned forward, her posture radiating an undeniable readiness to share every detail. David blinked, a slow realization dawning, and a similar quickening of his own expression mirrored Maria's sudden burst of enthusiasm. He nodded slowly, his eyes shining with a sudden, intense focus on her. "No kidding? I was there as well."
